LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT BENJAMIN JAYME'S (AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO DEMETRIO JAYME AND JUSTINA JAYME) MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE SECOND, SIXTH, SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND PUNITVE DAMAGE CLAIM IS GRANTED. AS A MATTER OF LAW, A LANDLORD RESPONDING TO A TENANT'S REQUEST TO REPLACE A ROOF BY HIRING A LICENSED ROOFING CONTRACTOR TO DO THE JOB DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A TORTIOUS BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING OR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS OR TRESPASS ON THE PART OF THE LANDLORD, NOTWITHSTANDING THAT THE ROOFER MAY HAVE CAUSED DEBRIS TO FALL INTO THE PREMISES AND MAY NOT HAVE DONE A GOOD JOB CLEANING UP. WITHOUT ANY REMAINING TORT CLAIMS, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST THE LANDLORD CANNOT SURVIVE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ARE SUSTAINED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents And To Seek Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf Andrew E. Reams And His Counsel Terence Mayo Of Mayo And Rogers; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-30-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Joshua J. Henderson, Esq., appeared for Defendant/Cross complainant & moving party: Benjamin Jayme
Terence Mayo/Mary Beth Mayo, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & opposing party: Andrew E. Reams

Ruling:
HEARING HELD on Defendant Benjamin Jayme's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Supplemental Request For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions. RULING AFTER HEARING: MOTION IS GRANTED-respond & produce within 10 days of notice of this order; sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. TERENCE O. MAYO OF MAYO & ROGERS, TEL. NO. 397-1515 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSHUA J. HENDERSON OF LORBER, GREENFIELD & POLITO, LLP. TEL. NO. 986-0688 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANTS/CROSS COMPLAINANTS BENJAMIN JAYME, AS SUCCESOR IN INTEREST TO DEMETRIO JAYME AND JUSTINA JAYME'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE SECOND, SIXTH, SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND PUNITVE DAMAGE CLAIM IS GRANTED. AS A MATTER OF LAW, A LANDLORD RESPONDING TO A TENANT'S REQUEST TO REPLACE A ROOF BY HIRING A LICENSED ROOFING CONTRACTOR TO DO THE JOB DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A TORTIOUS BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING OR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS OR TRESPASS ON THE PART OF THE LANDLORD, NOTWITHSTANDING THAT THE ROOFER MAY HAVE CAUSED DEBRIS TO FALL INTO THE PREMISES AND MAY NOT HAVE DONE A GOOD JOB CLEANING UP. WITHOUT ANY REMAINING TORT CLAIMS, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST THE LANDLORD CANNOT SURVIVE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ARE SUSTAINED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189 =301/PJB/PM
